mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 1.0% signals monetary accommodation that reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of future corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:current FEDFUNDS at 0.24% indicates deep accommodation
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in periods with FEDFUNDS < 1.0% do not significantly exceed baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in total nonfarm payroll employment above 150,000 persons signals robust labor demand and consumer income growth, driving positive equity returns over 5 trading days as market reprices growth expectations upward.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 1-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A joint contraction in retail sales and housing starts signals weakening consumer demand and construction activity, which reduces near-term corporate revenue expectations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both decline month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 448,086M and HOUST at 1,173K units
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of co-triggered instances, or interaction effect (joint decline) shows no incremental predictive power beyond univariate signals (F-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index declines below 105.0 (2017=100) signal supply-side weakness and reduced manufacturing capacity utilization, constraining corporate profit margins and producing negative equity returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with INDPRO < 105.0 do not significantly underperform baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate rises above 5.5% in a single month, signaling deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending power, which triggers negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as earnings growth expectations are revised downward.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.025 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPI rises above 3.5% year-over-year while the federal funds rate remains below 2.0%, indicating real rates are negative and the Fed is behind the inflation curve. This policy error produces negative equity returns over 60 trading days as market reprices terminal rate expectations.
regime_hint: monetary policy lag during inflation surge
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in co-triggered instances do not significantly underperform baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or interaction term adds no explanatory power beyond univariate models (F-test p > 0.15).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates to below 2.0% annualized, indicating the economy is approaching stall-speed growth. This regime shift produces negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as investors reprice terminal growth and earnings multiples compress.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in low-growth quarters do not significantly underperform high-growth quarters (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) rise above 1,300 thousand units annualized while retail sales (RSAFS) simultaneously accelerate month-over-month, signaling synchronized strength in residential and consumer demand. This joint expansion produces positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as cyclical growth expectations are revised upward.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in > 50% of co-triggered instances, or the interaction effect shows no incremental predictive power beyond univariate signals (F-test p > 0.12).
